subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the story of Janu and her connection with nature
Identify the protagonist's dreams and the obstacles she faces
Learn the importance of determination and self-belief
Recognize the role of the river as a source of inspiration and wisdom
Develop descriptive writing and empathy-based vocabulary
Understand the structure of a narrative and character growth

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Story
topics_covered: Overview of the story and the protagonist, Janu
teaching_strategy: Class discussion on favorite places in nature
Introduce Janu and her daily life by the river
Brainstorming: Can nature actually 'speak' to us?
student_activities: Share thoughts on what they would ask a river if it could talk
Predict Janu’s dream based on the title

lesson_plan.title: Janu’s Dream and Reality
topics_covered: Janu’s desire to go to school
The contrast between her brothers' lives and hers
teaching_strategy: Guided reading of the opening pages
Explanation of contextual vocabulary (e.g., chores, longing)
Discussion on gender roles in the story
student_activities: List the differences between Janu’s routine and her brothers’

lesson_plan.title: The River as a Mentor
topics_covered: The peace Janu finds at the water's edge
Sensory imagery used in the description
teaching_strategy: Teacher-led analysis of the river’s descriptions
Encourage students to close their eyes and visualize the scene
Identify metaphors used for the water
student_activities: Draw a scene of Janu sitting by the river
Write three adjectives describing the river's personality

lesson_plan.title: The Turning Point
topics_covered: The moment of courage and decision-making
Seeking permission or finding a way to learn
The internal conflict: Fear vs. Desire
teaching_strategy: Discussion on 'What would you do?' scenarios
Highlight the importance of Janu’s persistence
student_activities: Role-play a conversation between Janu and her parents
Draft a short 'inner monologue' for Janu

lesson_plan.title: Resolution and Themes
topics_covered: How Janu’s journey reaches a hopeful point
The theme of education as empowerment
The moral: Finding strength in one's environment
teaching_strategy: Read the conclusion of the story
Thematic discussion on 'The Right to Education'
student_activities: Write the main theme of the story in their notebooks
Write a 'Thank You' note from Janu to the river

lesson_plan.title: Language and Grammar
topics_covered: Adverbs of manner from the text
teaching_strategy: Grammar exercises based on story dialogues
student_activities: Convert Janu’s quotes into indirect speech
Complete a crossword puzzle using story keywords

teaching_aids: Audio clips of flowing water for atmosphere
Visual aids showing rural riverside life
teacher_tip: Focus on the emotional connection Janu has with the river; help students see the river not just as water, but as a character that gives Janu the courage her social environment lacks.
